feedback.text: One of my all time Favorit Racing Games is Need For Speed Rivals. It released in 2013, so its more than a decade old now. Its infamous for its "always-online" nature, which made this title sour to most gamers in the franchise, but now playing this game on PC in 2025 and using mods to fix some issues, its a blast to play. But as I just mentioned, its "always online" which means you need a connection to the games server trough internet to play this game. For now thoose servers are still up, for all versions of the game, even for the PS3 release. But what will we do when EA disables the servers? NFS Hot Pursuit, the more popular title from 2010 has no such issue, since A. It was re released in 2020 B. Is focused on offline play This issue took far greater significance, even going so far as player avoiding games with it like in the case of "Test Drive Unlimited: Solar Crown", a highly anticipated title many avoided by the sheer existence of an "always online" requirement. While I'm mentioning other games, my other Favorite Game was Overwatch, was since it doesn't exist anymore. The Version of 2016 is gone and was replaced with a Free-to-Play title, one missing major content like the "Archives" Event, in which you could play story missions with 4 other players. But what will I do with my beloved title?
